methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Herasight is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Michael Christensen that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 private and 3 public peers in Biotech & Health, including public comparables RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L, and private peers such as Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Stage-progression methodology is the sole applicable triangulation approach. Using total raised ($5M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $12M. Peer-median triangulation could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-stage comparable datapoints (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage peers identified across the 16 same-sector companies in scope). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 6.6x across three biotech tickers) could not be executed absent disclosed revenue for the target company.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M–$14M, centered on $12M. This estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single methodology and the absence of revenue data or tight peer multiples. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for this specific stage transition and the company's position relative to the identified private peer set. Further valuation refinement would benefit from revenue disclosure or identification of additional seed-stage biotech comparables.
